ingredients: Dressing: 3 tbsp cannabis olive oil, 1 tbsp red wine vinegar, 1 tsp oregano, salt
instructions: Combine vegetables and olives.
Whisk dressing and pour over. Toss.
Top with feta. Serve with crusty bread.
Roast tomatoes, onion and garlic at 400°F for 40 min.
Blend with broth until smooth.
Stir in cannabis cream and sugar; warm over low heat.
Season and top with fresh basil.

ingredients: 1 tsp each cumin, chili powder, garlic powder
Cannabis-lime dressing: juice of 2 limes + 2 tbsp cannabis olive oil
instructions: Season and grill chicken. Slice.
Build bowls: rice, beans, corn, chicken.
Drizzle with cannabis-lime dressing. Add toppings.

ingredients: Cannabis tahini: 3 tbsp tahini + 2 tbsp cannabis olive oil + lemon + water
instructions: Pulse chickpeas, onion, garlic, spices and flour. Refrigerate 30 min.
Form balls; fry at 350°F 3-4 min.
Make cannabis tahini dressing.
Fill flatbreads with falafel, salad and dressing.
Roast squash, onion and garlic at 400°F for 45 min.
Blend with broth until smooth.
Stir in cannabis cream and nutmeg; warm gently.
Season and top with pumpkin seeds.
Stir-fry rice in regular oil until slightly crispy.
Push aside; scramble eggs.
Add garlic and peas; toss everything. Add soy sauce.
Off heat: drizzle cannabis sesame oil. Garnish.
Season salmon and sear skin-side down 4 min until crispy.
Flip; cook 3 more min.
Melt cannabis butter with garlic and dill; spoon over fish.
Serve with capers and lemon.

ingredients: 3 tbsp cannabis extra virgin olive oil
instructions: Arrange alternating tomato and mozzarella slices.
Tuck basil leaves between.
Drizzle with cannabis olive oil and balsamic.
Finish with sea salt and pepper.

ingredients: 1 cup cooked chicken or black beans
instructions: Melt cannabis butter in a large pan.
Place tortilla; scatter cheese, filling and jalapeño over half.
Fold and cook 3 min per side until golden.
Cut into wedges and serve.

ingredients: Spring onions, bean sprouts, peanuts, lime
instructions: Stir-fry shrimp or tofu. Scramble eggs in same pan.
Add noodles, soy sauce, tamarind and sugar. Toss 2 min.
Add spring onions and bean sprouts.
Off heat: drizzle cannabis sesame oil. Serve with peanuts and lime.

ingredients: Toppings: ½ cup tomato sauce, 150g mozzarella, toppings of choice
Extra cannabis olive oil to finish
instructions: Mix dough; knead 8 min. Rise 1h.
Preheat oven to 475°F.
Stretch dough; add sauce, cheese and toppings.
Bake 10-12 min until golden.
Drizzle with cannabis olive oil before slicing.
Fry cumin seeds until they pop. Add onion, garlic and ginger.
Add tomatoes and spices. Add lentils and water; simmer 20-25 min.
Off heat: stir in cannabis ghee.
Season and garnish with coriander. Serve over rice.

ingredients: 2 cups dried black beans, soaked overnight
150g dried beef (carne seca), soaked and diced
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Drain beans, cover with fresh water, add smoked ribs and bay leaves. Simmer 1.5–2 hours until beans are tender.
In a skillet, cook bacon until crispy. Add sausage, dried beef, onion and garlic; sauté 5 min.
Transfer skillet contents to bean pot. Simmer 30 min more.
Remove from heat and stir in cannabis olive oil.
Serve over white rice with sautéed collard greens, farofa and orange slices.
Combine condensed milk, cocoa powder and cannabutter in a saucepan over medium-low heat.
Stir constantly until the mixture thickens and pulls away from the sides of the pan, about 10–15 min.
Transfer to a greased plate and let cool to room temperature.
Grease your hands with butter, roll into small balls and coat in chocolate sprinkles.
Place in mini cupcake liners and refrigerate until firm.
Pulse peanuts in a food processor until you get a fine, slightly oily crumb.
Add sugar, melted cannabutter and salt. Pulse a few more times until combined.
Press mixture firmly into small cylindrical molds or a lined baking pan.
Refrigerate 1 hour until set, then unmold.
Store in an airtight container at room temperature.
Season shredded chicken with cream cheese, salt and pepper. Set aside.
In a saucepan, bring broth and cannabutter to a boil. Add flour all at once, stirring vigorously until dough forms a ball. Let cool.
Take a portion of dough, flatten in your palm, add chicken filling, and shape into a teardrop.
Dip in beaten egg, then roll in breadcrumbs.
Deep fry at 350°F until golden brown, about 3–4 min. Drain on paper towels.

ingredients: 1 cup grated Parmesan or Minas cheese
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F.
Heat milk, cannabis olive oil and salt until just simmering. Pour over tapioca flour and stir until combined.
Let cool slightly, then mix in egg and cheese until smooth.
Roll into small balls and place on a lined baking sheet.
Bake 20–25 min until puffed and golden.

ingredients: Toppings: granola, sliced banana, strawberries, honey, shredded coconut
instructions: Blend frozen açaí puree, frozen banana, almond milk and cannabis coconut oil until thick and smooth.
Pour into a bowl.
Top with granola, sliced banana, strawberries, shredded coconut and a drizzle of honey.
Serve immediately.

ingredients: 500g firm white fish (cod or halibut), cut into chunks
instructions: Marinate fish in lime juice, salt and pepper for 30 min.
In a clay pot or deep pan, layer onion, peppers, tomatoes and fish.
Pour coconut milk and dendê oil over the top. Cover and simmer 20 min without stirring.
Remove from heat and gently stir in cannabis olive oil.
Garnish with cilantro. Serve over white rice.

ingredients: 1 cup hydrated tapioca starch (goma de tapioca)
Filling: shredded coconut, mozzarella or coalho cheese
instructions: Sift tapioca starch through a fine sieve to remove lumps.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat. Spread a thin layer of tapioca starch evenly across the pan.
When it turns translucent and holds together (about 1 min), add cheese and coconut to one half.
Fold and cook 1 min more until cheese melts.
Drizzle cannabis coconut oil and condensed milk on top. Serve immediately.

ingredients: ½ cup shredded coconut, plus more for rolling
instructions: Combine condensed milk, cannabutter and shredded coconut in a saucepan over medium-low heat.
Stir constantly until mixture thickens and pulls away from the sides, about 10–12 min.
Transfer to a greased plate and cool to room temperature.
Roll into small balls and coat in shredded coconut. Top each with a clove.
Place in mini cupcake liners and refrigerate until ready to serve.

ingredients: ½ cup cannabutter, cold and cubed
½ cup regular butter, cold and cubed
Filling: 2 cups shredded chicken, 1 onion, 2 garlic cloves, ½ cup green olives, ½ cup hearts of palm, 2 tbsp tomato paste, salt, pepper, parsley
instructions: Make dough: pulse flour, cannabutter, regular butter and egg yolks in a food processor. Add ice water until dough comes together. Wrap and refrigerate 30 min.
Make filling: sauté onion and garlic, add chicken, tomato paste, olives, hearts of palm, salt, pepper and parsley. Cook 5 min. Let cool.
Press ⅔ of dough into a greased pie dish. Add filling.
Roll remaining dough for top crust. Seal edges and brush with egg wash.
Bake at 375°F for 35–40 min until golden.
